requirement: Buyer can create a sourcing event for approved suppliers.
Buyer can manage and automate the sourcing event lifecycle, including notifications and workflows.
Supplier can collaborate with the buyer in real-time through the platform.
Buyer can evaluate and award contracts to suppliers based on a structured and user-friendly interface.
Buyer can manage and track sourcing events, including RFIs, RFQs, and RFPs.
Buyer can customize templates for sourcing events to promote consistency and reduce setup time.
Buyer can track and manage supplier responses and bids throughout the sourcing event lifecycle.
Buyer can analyze and compare supplier responses and bids to make informed decisions.
Buyer can award contracts to suppliers based on a structured and user-friendly interface.
Buyer can track and manage contract awards and supplier performance.
Buyer can initiate a sourcing request form with required information.
Supplier can participate and respond to sourcing events via email.
Evaluators can manually collate and evaluate supplier responses in a spreadsheet.
Procurement buyer can review and approve comparative schedules.
New suppliers can be onboarded by procurement buyer.
Supplier can be awarded by procurement buyer.
Users can conduct RFx (RFI, RFP, RFQ) and auctions (English reverse auction, Dutch reverse auction, Japanese reverse auction) events.
Advanced reporting capabilities offer actionable insights into sourcing events, supplier performance, and spend data.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event from scratch, using a template, or copying from a previous event.
Procurement Buyer can configure event settings, including event name, currency, terms and conditions, and sourcing team.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event and set evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can view and manage event timelines, details, and settings.
Procurement Buyer can select the type of sourcing event, including RFx stage, allow multiple responses, hide supplier responses, and allow suppliers to respond with attachments.
Procurement Buyer can attach terms and conditions or other relevant documents to the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can track cost avoidance and planned savings for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can create and manage sourcing events, including adding event owners, watchers, and evaluators.
Procurement Buyer can configure event details, including timelines, attachments, and forms.
Procurement Buyer can add and manage items, including quantity, price, and specifications.
Procurement Buyer can add and invite suppliers to sourcing events, including selecting from existing suppliers or adding new ones.
Procurement Buyer can add and manage evaluators for sourcing events.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with evaluation criteria and weights.
Procurement Buyer can assign evaluators for technical and commercial evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can launch a sourcing event and make it visible to suppliers.
Suppliers receive an invitation email with a link to the login page and system-generated OTP.
Suppliers must accept T&C before participating in the event.
Suppliers can access and participate in the event after accepting T&C.
Suppliers can resolve queries via the chat window.
Procurement Buyer can communicate with suppliers via the Messages Centre.
Procurement Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier can submit their responses to the event, including mandatory information.
Procurement Buyer can view the list of suppliers who were invited to the event and the ones who have responded.
Procurement Buyer can send email reminders to suppliers and extend the timeline of the bid.
Event can be auto-closed or manually ended by Procurement Buyer, after which the bidding closes and the event gets sealed.
Procurement Buyer can create a follow-up event for further negotiations with the supplier.
Evaluation process starts once responses are submitted, and Procurement Buyer can request internal users for evaluation.
Procurement Buyer can review and evaluate responses accordingly, and finalize the supplier to be awarded based on the supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event for approved suppliers.
Evaluators can add their scores and evaluations for a sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can review evaluator's evaluations for a sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can award the selected winner(s) in a sourcing event.
The system triggers the award approval process based on LOA and workflow configuration.
Once approvals are completed, the event is marked as awarded.
Procurement Buyer can flip the Sourcing event into a new Requisition or Purchase Order.
Procurement Buyer can create a new sourcing event from scratch, using a template, or copying from a previous event.
Procurement Buyer can configure event settings, including event name, currency, terms and conditions, and sourcing team/stakeholders.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can set evaluations for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can initiate the sourcing process by flipping a PR to a sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can select the option to skip the DOA approval workflow for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can view multiple sections to fill in sourcing information for the event.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with various options such as RFx stage, event type, and attachments.
Procurement Buyer can configure event details such as attachments, forms, and items.
Procurement Buyer can configure timelines specific to the event, including event time zone, start, and end dates.
Procurement Buyer can invite internal team members to participate in the sourcing process and assign tasks.
Procurement Buyer can manage event currencies and exchange rates, and allow suppliers to bid in different currencies.
Procurement Buyer can attach terms and conditions or other relevant documents for suppliers to accept.
Procurement Buyer can add event owner, watcher, and evaluator to the event team.
Procurement Buyer can allow suppliers to add multiple responses for the same item and hide supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can allow suppliers to respond with attachments in the message centre.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with details such as item information, supplier information, and evaluation criteria.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event by selecting from the Coupa supplier list or adding new suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can add evaluators for the sourcing event and assign weights to specific categories.
Procurement Buyer can launch the sourcing event and make it visible to suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can edit the sourcing event after launch, except for changes in currency, Terms and Conditions, and Event Type Settings.
Supplier receives an invitation email for participating in the event and viewing the event details.
Supplier accepts the T&C before participating in the event.
Supplier can access and participate in the event after accepting the T&C.
Supplier can resolve queries via the chat window.
Supplier submits their responses to the event.
Supplier can export the questionnaire to Excel and import responses from Excel.
Supplier can view the history of changes made to the form.
Supplier can save their responses and fill them up later.
Supplier can submit the response back to the Procurement Buyer.
Procurement Buyer can send email reminders to the suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can extend the timeline of the bid.
Event gets auto-closed or Procurement Buyer can manually end the event after which the bidding closes and the event gets sealed.
Procurement Buyer can create a follow-on event for RFP or RFQ or Auction.
Procurement Buyer can evaluate supplier responses and request internal users for evaluation.
Evaluators can add their scores and evaluations, and upload the file in the system.
Event status remains as Pending Evaluation until all invited Evaluators have submitted their evaluations.
Buyer can create an auction event from scratch, template, or by copying a previous event.
Buyer can configure auction-specific settings, including Docusign Envelope ID.
Buyer can select Automatic Bid Extensions to give suppliers time to react on a new best bid.
Buyer can set the number of minutes to extend the timeline upon arrival of a new best bid.
Buyer can select which cost to compare the new quote to, including total cost for the event or individual items.
Buyer can apply bidding rules to ensure fair and efficient price development.
Buyer can configure bidding rules to force suppliers to improve their bids by at least the specified change.
Buyer can configure bidding rules to specify the required improvement as a percent of the best bid or monetary amount.
Buyer can configure bidding rules to apply to individual items or lots.
Buyer can configure feedback to suppliers, including showing the currently best price per item or lot.
Buyer can configure feedback to suppliers, including showing the number of responses.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Buyer can award an auction to a selected supplier after approval.
Buyer can monitor the bidding process in the competitive chart.
Buyer can remove bids on behalf of suppliers and record the removal in the event history.
Buyer can prevent inadvertently incorrect submissions by showing a warning message when a supplier attempts to submit a quote which is more than 50% lower than the previous one.
Buyer can calculate and display savings definitions, including cost avoidance and planned savings.
System automatically calculates and enters negotiated savings into the Negotiated Savings field.
Buyer can view and manage sourcing settings and configurations.
Buyer can customize sourcing settings such as requiring suppliers to login, allowing suppliers to provide multiple responses, and sealing responses.
Buyer can enable or disable features such as sealed bidding, auto-population of supplier responses, and public events.
System enforces maximum number of line items, suppliers for RFx and auctions, and maximum number of suppliers for sourcing events.
Buyer can view and manage sourcing advantage events based on commodity.
Buyer can enable or disable features such as supplier community search and allow public events.
Buyer can run 'what if' scenarios to support the award decision.
Suppliers can access and participate in public sourcing events created by the company.
Coupa helps users find and connect with fellow users in the Coupa Community to share experiences of sourcing for a particular commodity.
Content groups are subgroups of users within a department and provide a way to manage individual users' access to content at the department level.
Automatically approve supplier participation requests for public sourcing events.
Publish public sourcing events on CSP.
Forward and reverse auctions are supported.
